candidates.evidence_quote: The Cheesecake Factory's comparable sales were negative 2.2% in the fourth quarter, down from 0.3% in the third quarter, demonstrating relative stability in comparison to the industry's sequential decline.
North Italia fourth quarter annualized AUVs totaled $7.6 million. Comparable sales declined 4%, reflecting broader industry sales trends.
Flower Child continued to perform exceptionally well and meaningfully outpaced the Fast Casual segment. Fourth quarter comparable sales increased 4%, for a two-year comp sales increase of 15%.
Q4 pricing was about 3.5% to 4%. and mix was a negative 1.8 and then traffic was the delta from that so as anticipated we still did see some some negative mix
with this year, Cheesecake will be about 3%. So we're bringing that down... we're investing in lower price points on an average basis. As I said, maybe that's 100 basis points of negative mix.
We said we would see some negative mix. We did. But in both December and now again in January, we're seeing incident rates year over year growing.
Adjusted restaurant level profit margins at the Cheesecake Factory increased 60 basis points year over year to 17.6%. with margin expansion also realized at North Italia and Flower Child.
cost of sales decreased 70 basis points... with the remainder primarily driven by favorable commodity costs and mix shift partially offset by higher beef costs.
we're aided there by while beef is higher, it's not as big of a piece for us. And dairy is measurably lower.
And dairy is measurably lower. So our market basket, I think, is aiding us in that endeavor.
We are modeling net total labor inflation of low to mid single digits when factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases, as well as other components of labor.
factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases, as well as other components of labor.
the continued improvement and retention, supporting labor productivity gains and wage leverage, as well as lower payroll taxes.
The remaining difference was primarily driven by higher group medical expenses, partially offset by the continued improvement and retention

With seven new restaurants opened in the fourth quarter, we finished the year with 25 new openings, delivering approximately 7% unit growth for 2025. Looking ahead, we expect to open as many as 26 restaurants in 2026.
Pre-opening costs were $9.4 million in the quarter compared to $7.6 million in the prior year period.
Adjusted annualized AUVs were $12.2 million for the quarter, supported by an off-premise sales mix of 22%, a slight improvement from recent quarters.
Total revenues were $961.6 million, inclusive of $17.3 million of gift card breakage revenue as a result of a change in historical redemption patterns.
Industry sales decelerated in the fourth quarter as reflected by the black box casual dining index declining sequentially by 410 basis points from the third quarter.
right now the weather to date through Q1 – we believe is about a 1% net impact, negative net impact on the entire quarter... I think we probably had 120 restaurants closed on the peak day.
continued pressure from sales transfer related to recently opened restaurants, as well as the lingering impact of the Los Angeles fires.
we're seeing evidence that there was truly the cannibalization and the fire as the comp is recovering... continued pressure from sales transfer related to recently opened restaurants

do you believe you've seen any fundamental change in the consumer spending backdrop at this point?... many factors in terms of whether you want to believe it's the K economy or the government shutdown.
Moving on to cheesecake rewards, we have made meaningful progress during the past 12 months, highlighted by strong membership growth and improved engagement.

notes: Single transcript event (CAKE Q4 FY2025 call), part 1 of 2; coined only from visible text.

Government shutdown and 'K economy' were mentioned as possible causes of soft consumer spending but folded under consumer_spending rather than coined as bound-to-instance names; government_shutdown is a reusable class but here it is only floated speculatively ('whether you want to believe it's...').

Skipped vague/non-driver items: tax refunds and gas prices (management explicitly said they see NO correlation), and bites/bowls menu innovation (a specific product initiative, captured via menu_mix/menu_pricing rather than a standalone brand driver).
